metadata.notes: Voice rewrite to Wingspan structured format — Opus, March 2026. Corrected per official rulebook, BGG FAQ, UltraBoardGames rules reference.

key_rulings.text: On your turn, place your Action token on one section of your Player Mat. You cannot repeat the same action you took on your previous turn.
A Seeker without resources may move up to 2 spaces. A Seeker carrying resources may move only 1 space.
If the attacker and defender have equal totals, the attacker wins.
If your Friendship is below 3, you cannot earn any trophies until you restore it to 3 or higher.
Once a trophy is earned, it cannot be lost, even if the condition that earned it is no longer met.
You may earn at most 1 trophy per turn, except during the Grand Finale.
To earn the Apple Delivery trophy, deliver exactly 4 apples to Castle Everfree in a single turn. Same for gems.
When you Seek, placing a resource on a space occupied by an opponent's Seeker grants you +1 Friendship per resource placed there.
Bake Pies: remove 2 apples, gain 2 pies. Conjure Spell: remove 2 gems, draw 1 spell. Power Up: remove 1 apple + 1 gem, draft a power-up tile.
The Pie Fight Victory trophy can be earned by winning as attacker OR defender. It is the only trophy that can be earned on another player's turn.

subtopics.title: Components
subtopics.content:
#### Board & Tiles
- **1 game board** (double-sided) — Hex-grid map of the Kingdom of Pomme with Castle Everfree at center, territories surrounding it, and base camp spaces around the outer edge. Pink-bordered spaces are **portals** that connect to each other across the board as shortcuts.
- **7 base camp tiles** — One per animal kingdom, marks where your seekers begin and retreat to after losing a pie fight.
- **16 power-up tiles** (8 Move, 8 Make) — Double-sided tiles drafted during the Make action. Each permanently upgrades one of your actions for the rest of the game.

#### Miniatures & Tokens
- **14 seeker miniatures** — 2 per animal kingdom (7 kingdoms total). Your pair of adventurers on the board.
- **28 trophy tokens** (4 per player) — Placed on the Trophy Track as you earn trophies.
- **7 friendship tokens** — One per player, tracked on the Friendship Track (range 0-8, start at 3).
- **7 pie tokens** — One per player, tracked on the Pie Track (range 0-10, start at 3).
- **7 action tokens** — Placed on your Player Mat each turn to show which action you chose.

#### Resources
- **24 apple tokens** — Red acrylic tokens. One of the two resource types to collect and deliver.
- **25 gem tokens** — Blue acrylic tokens. The other resource type.
- **12 quest tokens** — Placed on the board when you Seek. Landing on one triggers a quest.

#### Cards & Dice
- **12 quest cards** — Each presents two options with different friendship consequences (generous vs. greedy).
- **30 magic spell cards** — One-time-use abilities for various effects. Can also be attached to pie fights for extra strength (adds 2-5 to your combat total).
- **8 personality cards** — Secret cards dealt at setup. Each modifies a specific trophy requirement, making it easier to earn.
- **5 custom dice** — Red dice generate apples, blue dice generate gems, gold dice generate quest tokens. Each face shows a region where you place the resource.
- **6 player mats** — Track your action choices and power-up slots.
- **2 pie fight dials** — Secret dials for setting your pie bid during combat. Set privately, reveal simultaneously.

#### Solo Mode
- **1 Automountie board + tokens** — AI opponent for solo play.

#### Other
- **1 setup tile** — Determines where starting resources get placed on the board.
- **1 painting guide + achievement sheet**
- **2 rulebooks** (multiplayer + solo)

subtopics.title: Game Structure & Setup
subtopics.content:
#### How the Game Works
- **Goal** — Race to earn **4 trophies** out of 8 possible categories to trigger the endgame.
- **Actions** — Each turn, pick one of three actions: Move, Seek, or Make.
- **Action restriction** — You **cannot repeat the same action** you took last turn.
- **Core loop** — Explore the Kingdom of Pomme, collect apples and gems, deliver them to Castle Everfree, complete quests, engage in pie fights, and manage your friendship.
- **Friendship lock-out** — If friendship drops below 3, you are locked out of earning any trophies until you restore it.

#### Setting Up the Board
1. **Unfold the game board** and place it in the center of the table.
2. **Pick your animal kingdom** — Grab 2 matching seeker miniatures, 1 base camp tile, 1 action token, 4 trophy tokens, and 1 player mat.
3. **Place your base camp tile** on the board in the position shown for your player count.
4. **Put both seekers** on your base camp — they always start together.
5. **Set your friendship token** on **level 3** of the Friendship Track.
6. **Set your pie token** on **level 3** of the Pie Track.
7. **Shuffle the quest cards** and place them face-down near the board.
8. **Shuffle the spell cards** — Deal **1 spell** to each player (keep secret), place the rest face-down as a draw pile.
9. **Separate the power-up tiles** into Move and Make piles, then shuffle each pile face-down.
10. **Place the setup tile** on Castle Everfree, then flip it — this tells you where to put starting resources on the board.
11. **Deal 1 personality card** to each player (keep secret — reveal later for advantage).
12. **Pick a first player** randomly — play proceeds clockwise.

#### Checkpoint
- Each player has: 2 seekers on their base camp, friendship at 3, pies at 3, 1 spell card in hand, 1 secret personality card, 4 trophy tokens ready to place, a player mat, and an action token.
- The board has resources scattered on it from the setup tile.

subtopics.title: Turn Flow
subtopics.content:
#### Your Turn
- **Choose an action** — Place your action token on **Move**, **Seek**, or **Make** on your player mat.
- **Action restriction** — You cannot choose the same action you took on your previous turn.
- **Trophy check** — After resolving the action, check if you earned a trophy (max **1 trophy per turn**).
- **Pass** — Play proceeds clockwise.

#### Action 1: Move
- **Move both seekers** independently across the hex grid.
- **Without resources** — Up to **2 spaces** per seeker.
- **Carrying resources** — Only **1 space** per seeker, but resources travel with you.
- **Portals** — Pink-bordered spaces are adjacent to each other. Moving between portals costs 1 movement (great shortcuts).
- **Picking up resources** — Entering a space with resources automatically picks them up. Movement then drops to 1 space.
- **Delivering to Castle Everfree** — Carrying resources into Castle Everfree deposits all of them. Deliver **4 apples in a single turn** for the Apple Delivery trophy, or **4 gems** for the Gem Delivery trophy (both seekers contribute). After delivery, seekers return to base camp and you get **rejuvenated** (gain 1 spell OR 2 pies, action token removed from mat so any action is available next turn).
- **Entering an occupied space** — Moving onto an opponent's seeker triggers a **pie fight** immediately.
- **Quests** — After all movement, resolve any quest tokens on spaces where your seekers ended up.

#### Action 2: Seek
- **Roll the dice** shown on your player mat's Seek section.
- **Red dice** — Place 1 apple token on any space in the indicated region.
- **Blue dice** — Place 1 gem token on any space in the indicated region.
- **Gold dice** — Place 1 quest token on any **unoccupied** space in the indicated region.
- **Friendship bonus** — Placing a resource on a space occupied by an opponent's seeker grants **+1 friendship per resource** placed there.

#### Action 3: Make (Choose One)
- **Bake Pies** — Remove **2 apples** from your seekers' spaces, gain **2 pies** on the Pie Track (max 10).
- **Conjure Spell** — Remove **2 gems**, draw **1 magic spell card** (no hand limit).
- **Power Up** — Remove **1 apple + 1 gem**, draw top 3 tiles from either the Move or Make power-up pile. Keep 1, return the other 2 to the bottom. Place the tile on your player mat — **permanently upgrades** that action.

subtopics.title: Pie Fights, Quests & Friendship
subtopics.content:
#### Pie Fights (Combat)
**Trigger** — A pie fight happens whenever your seeker moves onto a space occupied by an opponent's seeker.

**Resolution steps:**
1. **Attacker loses 1 friendship immediately** — This is the cost of aggression, happens before anything else.
2. **Set dials secretly** — Both players set their pie fight dial (0 up to your current pie total). For each seeker in the fight, you may attach **1 magic spell card** (adds 2-5 to your total).
3. **Reveal simultaneously** — Both players flip dials.
4. **Highest total wins** — Ties go to the **attacker**.
5. **Pies spent** — All bid pies are removed from your Pie Track. Spell cards used are discarded.
6. **Loser retreats** — Loser's seekers return to base camp, dropping all carried resources on the battle space.
7. **Winner earns Pie Fight Victory trophy** — If not already earned.
8. **Loser gets rejuvenated** — Gain 1 spell OR 2 pies, action token removed from mat.

**Key details:**
- You CAN fight with 0 pies (risky, but sometimes worth it).
- **Pie Fight Victory** is the **only trophy** earnable on another player's turn (as a defending winner).
- Spell cards add 2-5 to your total — powerful combat insurance that deters attacks.

#### Quests
**Trigger** — After finishing your Move action (including any pie fights), check if either seeker is on a space with a quest token.

**Resolution:**
1. **Remove** the quest token from the board.
2. **Draw** 1 quest card.
3. **Choose one option:**
   - **Option 1** — Usually the generous path (smaller reward, gain friendship).
   - **Option 2** — Usually the greedy path (bigger reward, lose friendship).
   - **"No Thanks"** — Return card to bottom of deck. Does **not** count toward Quest trophy.

- Completing **2 quests** (Option 1 or 2) earns the **Quest trophy**.

#### The Friendship Track
- **Range** — 0 to 8. Everyone starts at **3**.

**Friendship goes up when you:**
- Place resources on an opponent's seeker space during Seek (+1 per resource)
- Choose the generous option on quest cards
- Use certain spell effects

**Friendship goes down when you:**
- Initiate a pie fight (-1 immediately)
- Choose the greedy option on quest cards

**CRITICAL RULE — Friendship lock-out:**
- If friendship drops **below 3**, you are **completely locked out of earning ANY trophies** until you restore it to 3 or higher.
- This is the game's primary anti-aggression mechanism.
- Do not pick fights until you have built a friendship cushion above 3.

- Reaching friendship **level 8** earns the **Friendship trophy**.

subtopics.title: Trophies & Endgame
subtopics.content:
#### The 8 Trophy Categories
- **Goal** — Earn **4 trophies** to trigger the endgame.
- **Limit** — At most **1 trophy per turn** (except during the Grand Finale).

1. **Friendship Trophy** — Reach level **8** on the Friendship Track.
2. **Pie Trophy** — Reach level **8** on the Pie Track.
3. **Power Ups Trophy** — Have **both** a Move power-up AND a Make power-up on your player mat simultaneously.
4. **Spell Trophy** — Hold **3 or more** magic spell cards in hand at once.
5. **Apple Delivery Trophy** — Deliver exactly **4 apples** to Castle Everfree in a **single turn** (both seekers contribute).
6. **Gem Delivery Trophy** — Deliver exactly **4 gems** to Castle Everfree in a **single turn** (both seekers contribute).
7. **Quest Trophy** — Complete **2 quest cards** by choosing Option 1 or Option 2 ("No Thanks" does not count).
8. **Pie Fight Victory Trophy** — **Win** a pie fight as attacker or defender. Only trophy earnable on someone else's turn.

**Key rules:**
- **Trophies are permanent** — Once earned, a trophy stays even if the condition is no longer met.
- **Friendship lock-out** — If friendship is **below 3**, you cannot earn any trophies until restored.

#### Personality Cards
- **Secret modifier** — Each personality card modifies one trophy's requirement, making it easier to earn.
- **Timing** — Reveal at any point during the game. Best revealed the moment you can **immediately claim** an unexpected trophy.

#### Endgame (Grand Finale)
- **Trigger** — The moment any player places their **4th trophy**, the Grand Finale begins.
- **Finish the round** — Complete the current round so every player gets equal turns.
- **Trophy limit lifted** — During the Grand Finale, the 1-trophy-per-turn limit is removed.

#### Winning
1. **Most trophies** wins.
2. Tied on trophies? **Higher friendship** breaks the tie.
3. Still tied? **More pies** remaining.
4. Still tied? **Shared victory**.

#### Solo Mode (Automountie)
- AI opponent controlled by action tokens and a decision flowchart.
- Earns trophies using the same rules — your goal is to reach 4 trophies first.

subtopics.title: Beginner Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Opening Moves (Turns 1-3)
- **Turn 1 — Seek** — Roll dice and place resources near your seekers for easy pickup. Place at least a couple on opponents' seeker spaces for **+1 friendship each** (free friendship while seeding the board).
- **Turn 2 — Move** — Send seekers to collect the resources you just placed. Carrying resources limits movement to 1 space, so keep resources close to your starting position.
- **Turn 3 — Make** — Spend 1 apple + 1 gem to Power Up. Draw 3 tiles from either pile, keep the best one, place it on your mat. Getting your first power-up early pays dividends all game.

#### The Fastest Path to 4 Trophies
1. **Power Ups** — Get both a Move AND a Make power-up (1 apple + 1 gem each, across 2 Make actions). Most reliable trophy — depends entirely on your own actions.
2. **Friendship** — Reach level 8. Start at 3, gain +1 each time you place resources on opponents' spaces during Seek. Generous quest choices help too.
3. **Spells** — Hold 3 spell cards simultaneously. Each Conjure Spell costs 2 gems and draws 1 card. Three Make actions gets you there.
4. **Quest** — Complete 2 quests by landing on quest tokens and choosing Option 1 or 2 (not "No Thanks").

#### Key Rule: Don't Drop Below Friendship 3
- If friendship drops below 3, you are **completely locked out of earning trophies** — all of them, not just Friendship.
- Every pie fight costs 1 friendship. Starting at 3, a single unprepared fight drops you to 2.
- Build friendship above 3 before considering combat.

#### When to Fight
- **Friendship at 5 or higher** — You can absorb the -1 without lock-out danger.
- **Holding spell cards** — Each adds 2-5 to your total (massive swing).
- **Opponent carrying resources** — If you win, they drop everything on the battle space.
- **You're the attacker** — Ties go to the attacker, so close matchups favor the initiator.

#### Seek Friendship Strategy
- Placing resources on opponents' spaces during Seek gives **+1 friendship per resource** (free).
- You populate the board (necessary anyway) AND gain friendship simultaneously.
- Target occupied spaces whenever possible — gaining 2-3 friendship per Seek is realistic.

#### Action Restriction Planning
- You cannot repeat the same action twice in a row.
- Plan in natural pairs: Seek then Move (collect what you placed), Move then Make (spend what you collected), Make then Seek (refill the board).
- The Seek-Move-Make cycle is the backbone of your game plan.

#### Common Mistakes
- **Fighting at friendship 3** — One fight drops you to 2 and locks you out of all trophies. Build friendship first.
- **Forgetting the action restriction** — You cannot Seek twice in a row. Always think one turn ahead.
- **Ignoring spells in combat** — Spell cards add 2-5 to your pie fight total. Holding 3 spells earns the Spell trophy AND makes you a combat threat.
- **Delivering fewer than 4 resources** — You need exactly 4 apples (or 4 gems) to Castle Everfree in a single turn. Delivering 3 gets nothing. Use both seekers to hit the threshold.
- **Revealing personality card too early** — Your personality card lets you claim a trophy under modified conditions. Wait for the optimal moment — the element of surprise is half its value.

subtopics.title: First-Game Walkthrough
subtopics.content:
#### Your First Game of My Little Scythe

**After Setup**: Each player should have 2 seekers on their base camp, friendship at 3, pies at 3, 1 spell card in hand, 1 secret personality card, and 4 trophy tokens ready to place. Resources from the setup tile should be scattered across the board.

**Turn 1 — Seek**
*Action*: Roll your dice (e.g., 2 red apples and 2 blue gems). Place those resources on the board.
*Smart move*: Put some near your seekers for easy pickup. Place at least 1 on an opponent's seeker space for +1 friendship, bumping you to 4. Free friendship on turn 1.

**Turn 2 — Move** (cannot repeat Seek)
*Action*: Move both seekers toward the resources you just placed. Without cargo, each seeker moves up to 2 spaces. Entering a resource space picks it up automatically.
*Remember*: Once carrying resources, movement drops to 1 space.

**Turn 3 — Make** (cannot repeat Move)
*Action*: Spend 1 apple + 1 gem to Power Up. Draw 3 tiles from the Move pile, keep the best, return the other 2.
*Progress*: One more power-up (from the other pile) earns the **Power Ups trophy**.

**Turn 4 — Seek** (cannot repeat Make)
*Action*: Roll dice again. Place resources near seekers and on opponents' spaces for more friendship. Gold dice place quest tokens on unoccupied spaces.

**Turn 5 — Move**
*Action*: Collect resources again. If a quest token is along your path, land on it to draw a quest card and choose an option. That counts as quest 1 of 2 toward the **Quest trophy**.

**Turn 6 — Make**
*Action*: Spend 1 apple + 1 gem for a second Power Up from the Make pile. You now have both Move and Make power-ups on your mat.
*Trophy earned*: **Power Ups trophy** — place your first trophy token. That is 1 of 4.

**Mid-Game Goals**
- Build friendship toward 8 (Friendship trophy)
- Accumulate 3 spell cards in hand (Spell trophy)
- Complete a second quest (Quest trophy)
- Watch for delivery opportunities (4 apples or 4 gems to Castle Everfree in one turn)

**The Home Stretch**
- At 3 trophies, you are one away from triggering the endgame.
- Consider revealing your personality card if it makes a trophy easier to claim.
- Placing your 4th trophy triggers the **Grand Finale** — finish the round for equal turns, then most trophies wins.

subtopics.title: Advanced Strategy
subtopics.content:
#### Trophy Rush vs. Slow Build
- **Rush strategy** — Target the easiest trophies (Power Ups, Pie Fight Victory, quests) and race to 4 before opponents set up their engines.
- **Slow build** — Accumulate resources, upgrade both actions, then claim 3-4 trophies in rapid succession over consecutive turns.
- **Risk** — Against experienced players, slow build is dangerous because someone may Grand Finale before you are ready.

#### Friendship Track Mastery
- Reaching level 8 earns the **Friendship trophy** and keeps all other trophy paths open.
- **Best climbing method** — Seek generously. Every resource placed on an opponent's seeker space grants +1 friendship for free.
- Combine with generous quest choices (Option 1) to realistically hit 8 by mid-game without slowing your resource engine.

#### Delivery Trophy Planning
- **Requirement** — 4 apples (or 4 gems) to Castle Everfree in a single turn.
- **Setup** — Both seekers must carry resources and converge on the castle in the same Move action.
- **Preparation** — Place resources in a line between your seekers and the castle during Seek, collect on one Move, deliver on the next.
- **Move power-ups** that extend movement range make deliveries much more consistent — prioritize these if going for a delivery trophy.

#### Combat as a Trophy Tool
- **Cost** — 1 friendship per fight.
- **Ideal attack conditions:**
  - Friendship at **5 or higher** (absorbs the -1 safely).
  - Holding **1-2 spell cards** for dial boosting (each adds 2-5).
  - Target is **carrying valuable resources** you can scoop up after winning.
- **Ties go to the attacker** — Even matching your opponent's investment wins.
- **Advanced trick** — If low on pies but holding strong spells, bid 0 pies and rely entirely on spell value.

#### Power-Up Tile Priorities
- **Move power-ups** — Extra movement or carrying resources without speed penalty. Game-changing for delivery trophies and reaching quest tokens faster.
- **Make power-ups** — Reduced costs or bonus effects on Bake Pies / Conjure Spell. Stretch resources further.
- Prioritize whichever type aligns with your trophy plan.
- Having one of each type earns the **Power Ups trophy** automatically.

#### Personality Card Timing
- **What it does** — Modifies one trophy requirement to make it easier.
- **Optimal reveal** — The moment you can **immediately claim a trophy** opponents were not expecting.
- **Revealing early** gives opponents time to adjust. Revealing at the perfect moment can push you to 4 trophies before anyone responds.
